channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🌏 Expanding frontiers: China’s military push beyond the first island chain | ASPI Strategist
item.description: China’s defence and security presence beyond the Western Pacific is set to intensify over the next decade, expanding its access, influence and operational reach across the Indo-Pacific. In the Southwest Pacific, Beijing is likely to ...

item.title: 📍 Latin America’s Anti-Women Movement Is Spreading | Foreign Policy
item.description: Chile's president José Antonio Kast is following the regressive examples set elsewhere in the region.

item.title: 🏛️ What Congress Could Do to Stop the War | Foreign Policy
item.description: Republicans are declining to use their power of the purse.

item.title: 🌍 The Iran War Is Tearing Trump’s Coalition Apart | Foreign Policy
item.description: MAGA is not necessarily the same thing as America First.

item.title: 📍 The Disposable Oligarchs | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Why wealthy elites come to regret their bargains with authoritarians.

item.title: 📍 Why aircraft carriers are the best (and worst) place for laser weapons | Defense News
item.description: High-energy laser weapons are a natural fit for large, power-rich aircraft carriers — with limits.

item.title: 🏛️ What we know about the US military’s new joint laser weapon system | Defense News
item.description: The Army-Navy effort aims to produce a containerized 150-kilowatt high-energy laser weapon to counter incoming cruise missiles.

item.title: 🏛️ A concern for Australia: Britain looks unserious about naval defence | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australia should have a big concern. Britain, a country that Australia is relying on for its future submarine force and much else under AUKUS, looks seriously unserious about naval defence. Quite apart from the challenge ...

item.title: 📍 MP7 Personal Defense Weapon Just Went Viral In Hands Of Tailored-Suit Wearing Agent | The War Zone
item.description: The cool under pressure agent yanked the MP7 from a discreet backpack after a shooter tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Dinner. The post MP7 Personal Defense Weapon Just Went Viral In Hands Of Tailored-Suit Wearing Agent appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Taking the Three Seas Initiative to the next level | Atlantic Council
item.description: The thirteen-member-state organization should transition from a loose platform into an operational entity with a permanent front office. The post Taking the Three Seas Initiative to the next level appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 Russia’s Stealthy S-71K Air-Launched Missile Seen In New Detail | The War Zone
item.description: The missile aims to be a less expensive, but still survivable standoff weapon option that can be built in large numbers. The post Russia’s Stealthy S-71K Air-Launched Missile Seen In New Detail appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 The U.S. Shouldn’t Rule the Seas Forever | Foreign Policy
item.description: Why the United States can no longer guarantee freedom of navigation—and why it doesn’t need to.

item.title: 🌍 Iran Offers to Reopen the Strait of Hormuz | Foreign Policy
item.description: But only if the United States postpones nuclear talks and lifts its own naval blockade.

item.title: 🌍 Iran Crisis: Pakistan’s Prominence and India’s Strategic Silence | The Diplomat
item.description: While Pakistan has stepped up to try and mediate between the U.S. and Iran, India has taken a much quieter approach.

item.title: 📍 Armenia's Specters of Peace and War | RAND
item.description: Pivotal parliamentary elections are coming in June, and Armenia's young democracy faces a stirring test between the ghosts of its history and a hopeful bet on a stable future.

item.title: 📍 CSO Saltzman: Focus space acquisition on ‘minimum viable capabilities’ | Breaking Defense
item.description: In his first Commander’s Note of 2026, Gen. Chance Saltzman urges close collaboration between the Space Force acquisition and operational corps to speed delivery of new capabilities.

item.title: 📍 Australian defence depends on stronger northern systems | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Northern Australia will decide whether Australia will sustain combat power where it matters most or fail to do so before tempo can be maintained. Policy has settled on the importance of geography. Execution will determine ...

item.title: 🌍 Prospects Dimming On Iran-U.S. Deal To Open Strait, End War | The War Zone
item.description: Tehran has delivered a new offer to Washington that is unlikely to move the needle as blockade aims to cripple Iran's oil infrastructure. The post Prospects Dimming On Iran-U.S. Deal To Open Strait, End War appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🕵️ Can King Charles’s state visit to the US help mend the special relationship? | Atlantic Council
item.description: The British monarch’s visit comes as relations between Washington and London are at their lowest point in decades. The post Can King Charles’s state visit to the US help mend the special relationship? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Three scenarios for the USMCA’s review—and why auto manufacturers should prepare now | Atlantic Council
item.description: Auto manufacturers should take steps to make their supply chains and investments more resilient to shocks resulting from significant changes to the agreement or its expiration. The post Three scenarios for the USMCA’s review—and why auto manufacturers should prepare now appeared…

item.title: 📍 Risk as design input: The Eight-Factor Risk Framework for cognitive operations | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] The Eight-Factor Risk Framework’s theoretical foundation describes the three zones every audience holds simultaneously.

item.title: 🏛️ US Navy’s unmanned MQ-25A Stingray notches first successful test flight | Defense News
item.description: The U.S. Navy and Boeing conducted the Stingray's first test flight on Saturday, showing its ability to taxi, take off, fly and land autonomously.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy’s MQ-25 Stingray carrier drone completes first test flight | Breaking Defense
item.description: The aircraft took off from Boeing’s facility at MidAmerica Airport in Mascoutah, Ill., for an approximately two-hour flight.

item.title: ⚛️ US Air Force looks to launch cheap missiles from cargo aircraft | Defense News
item.description: The U.S. Air Force wants inexpensive, long-range missiles that can be launched in mass volleys by cargo aircraft.

item.title: 🏴 Rheinmetall lands $1.2 billion German future soldier contract | Breaking Defense
item.description: “In total, the German Bundestag has approved €1.3 billion for the project a few days ago, so further call-offs are expected,” said the European manufacturer.

item.title: 🌏 US Army eyes a heavier, hybrid-powered Infantry Squad Vehicle | Defense News
item.description: While the original ISV was designed to rapidly move a nine-soldier squad across rugged terrain, the ISV-H is built to do more than just transport troops.

item.title: 🏴 Evidence Of Ukraine Using AIM-120C-8 Missiles Emerges | The War Zone
item.description: Wreckage imagery indicates Ukraine is fielding near-top-tier AIM-120C-8s, which are employable via F-16s and NASAMS launchers. The post Evidence Of Ukraine Using AIM-120C-8 Missiles Emerges appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Confronting the Past and Present, Lessons from Chornobyl | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary and analysis from across the political spectrum only for War on the Rocks members. Each Monday, we bring you a curated selection of articles from Ukrainian media offering insight into how Ukrainians…

item.title: 📍 Psychedelics, Policy, and Public Opinion: Q&A with Beau Kilmer | RAND
item.description: Beau Kilmer oversees RAND's research on psychedelic use and policy which informs conversations on the topic and helps decisionmakers think through all the different policy options and to understand the potential trade-offs.

item.title: 🌏 Beijing Is Using Influencers to Burnish Its Image | Foreign Policy
item.description: “Chinamaxxing” has become an online phenomenon.
